element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational offensive tool; available from start
Earned by counter-punching at precise moments; stores up to 3 stars
High-damage attack requiring star accumulation; primary knockout tool
Stamina/exhaustion system; depletes on hit, block, or whiffed punch
Triggered when heart meter reaches zero; restricts offense but allows defense
Round-ending condition; third knockdown = TKO loss
Each opponent has fixed attack sequence requiring trial-and-error discovery
Increased complexity and speed in attack sequences
Returning opponents from earlier circuits with new attacks forcing strategy revision
Requires point accumulation; unavailable for final boss fights
Highly skilled final opponent; loss ends game

typical_relief_triggers: Heart meter recovery during opponent's recovery phase
Opponent pattern recognition leading to confident counter-attack
typical_tension_triggers: Heart meter depletion forcing exhaustion state
Second knockdown in a round (one away from TKO loss)
Loss of accumulated stars on being hit
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Fight-by-fight progression with escalating opponent complexity; three rounds per bout with tension peaks at knockdowns; relief through successful pattern exploitation and recovery phases

difficulty_curve.early_game: Minor Circuit introduces core mechanics (jabs, blocks, dodges) against simple opponents like Glass Joe; low punishment for learning
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Major Circuit increases opponent AI complexity and attack speed; introduces more demanding pattern recognition; rematch losses cause ranking fallback
difficulty_curve.late_game: World Circuit reprises earlier opponents with new attacks requiring strategy revision; cumulative mastery demanded; Dream Fight against Mike Tyson/Mr. Dream as ultimate skill test
notable_spikes: Transition from Minor to Major Circuit (opponent speed/pattern complexity)
World Circuit opponents with new variants of familiar patterns
Dream Fight final boss (highest skill opponent)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game organized into Minor (3 opponents), Major (4 opponents), World (6 opponents), and Dream Fight; each circuit requires championship victory to advance
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Glass Joe (Minor) → Von Kaiser (Minor) → Bald Bull (Minor) → King Hippo (Major)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each opponent follows a fixed attack sequence requiring trial-and-error learning; success depends on memorization and precise timing rather than real-time reaction
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All opponents (e.g., Bald Bull's Bull Charge attack has predictable timing/vulnerability window)

notable_level_patterns.description: Three opponents reappear in World Circuit with new attacks, forcing strategy revision after earlier mastery
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Bald Bull, King Hippo, and one additional opponent return with enhanced movesets

notable_level_patterns.description: Each fight spans up to three rounds; TKO triggered at three knockdowns in one round; knockout possible at any time; decision-based victory requires point accumulation
First loss to ranked opponent grants rematch; subsequent losses cause ranking fallback (1-2 places depending on circuit); third loss or Dream Fight loss ends game
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Losing to Major Circuit opponent drops ranking by 1 place in that circuit
